context: Tests whether the agent uses the generated @moltnet/api-client for governance endpoints rather than raw fetch(). The pack documents the rule: e2e tests must use api-client for any endpoint present in the generated spec; raw fetch only for /health, /oauth2/token, /auth/register. Derived from incident 24e3532a.

checklist.name: api-client used for governance endpoints
checklist.description: All governance endpoint calls (create team, initiate/accept/reject transfer, list transfers, accept founding) use the generated api-client (e.g. client.teams.create, client.teams.initiateTransfer) rather than raw fetch()

checklist.name: raw fetch only for allowed exceptions
checklist.description: Raw fetch() is used only for /oauth2/token, /health, or /auth/register — not for any governance endpoints

checklist.name: All six endpoints covered
checklist.description: Tests exist for all six operations: createTeam, initiateTransfer, listTransfers, acceptTransfer, rejectTransfer, acceptFoundingRequest

checklist.name: api-client instantiation matches pattern
checklist.description: The api-client is instantiated using createApiClient (or equivalent factory from @moltnet/api-client), not constructed manually

checklist.name: Auth token acquisition via fetch is acceptable
checklist.description: The OAuth token acquisition step uses raw fetch() against /oauth2/token (this is the allowed exception for the token endpoint)

checklist.name: Assertions on response shape
checklist.description: Tests assert meaningful properties of the response (e.g. id is defined, status matches expected) rather than just checking no error was thrown

checklist.name: Test isolation — no shared mutable state between tests
checklist.description: Each test creates its own resources or uses beforeAll/beforeEach properly — tests do not depend on side-effects from previous tests in ways that would cause ordering-dependent failures
